title: When Someone Shares Information: - Ask for Clarity:
subtitle: Test the Truth
description:
When someone shares information:
- Ask for clarity: "Where did you hear this?" or "What’s the source?"
- Cross-check: Verify their claims with other evidence.
- Stay neutral: Don’t assume truth or falsehood—investigate first.

Example: A colleague makes a bold claim in a meeting. Instead of accepting it outright, request data or examples.

faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

howTo.title: Ask for Clarity
howTo.summary: When someone shares information, ask questions like 'Where did you hear this?' or 'What’s the source?' to encourage evidence-based discussions.

howTo.title: Cross-check Claims
howTo.summary: Verify the shared information by checking against other evidence. Assess the credibility of the source and the context of the claim.

howTo.title: Normalize Source Requests
howTo.summary: Make asking for sources a standard practice in discussions. Use neutral language to reduce defensiveness and foster a culture of verification.

howTo.title: Implement Triage Routine
howTo.summary: Establish a quick triage routine for evaluating sources. Consider sample size, question wording, and potential conflicts of interest.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your verification efforts and results in the Brali LifeOS app to track progress and improve your source-checking habits. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/verify-claims-ask-for-sources/.
